education.special_skills_qualifications:
Technology & Library Systems:
- Comfortable with integrated library systems and catalog interfaces; completed coursework and hands-on labs using Koha and Evergreen ILS, including searching, placing holds, checking materials in and out, renewing items, and updating patron records.
- Proficient with common office and productivity software, including Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Publisher) and Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Forms). I regularly create flyers, simple brochures, and reading lists for community and church events.
- Strong internet research skills; I often help older family members and neighbors navigate online forms (DMV renewals, basic benefits applications, and simple troubleshooting like browser issues or password resets).

Customer Service & Communication:
- Over 10 years of experience in customer-facing roles (retail bookstore, front-desk receptionist, and after-school tutoring), which has made me very comfortable greeting people, answering questions, and de-escalating tense situations calmly and respectfully.
- I’m used to working with people of all ages, from young children to seniors, and adjusting my communication style accordingly—using clear, simple language without being condescending.
- Strong written communication skills: I have experience writing clear emails, informational handouts, and step-by-step instructions for non-technical users.

Youth & Community Programming:
- Volunteered for four summers (2018–2022) assisting with a local church’s children’s reading circle and summer program. I helped select age-appropriate books, prepared simple crafts connected to the stories, tracked reading logs, and gave small weekly “book talks” to get kids excited about reading.
- Comfortable reading aloud to groups of children and leading simple activities (coloring, basic STEM crafts, story-related games). I have experience managing groups of 8–15 children at a time with a focus on positive reinforcement and clear expectations.

Organization & Attention to Detail:
- Very detail-oriented with shelving and filing: at my previous job at a used bookstore, I was responsible for alphabetizing and re-shelving inventory and catching misfiled books. I developed the habit of double-checking call numbers, titles, and authors before finalizing shelving.
- Strong habit of keeping written notes and checklists. For example, during my Library Technology certificate, I maintained detailed binders of procedures for tasks like item processing, spine labeling, and basic cataloging, which my classmates sometimes used as reference.

Language & Interpersonal Skills:
- Native English speaker with conversational Spanish skills (able to handle basic questions about hours, locations, and simple directions; can help Spanish-speaking patrons with very simple forms if they speak slowly).
- Patient and empathetic listener; I grew up helping my younger brother, who has mild learning differences, with reading and homework, which taught me to break tasks into smaller steps and celebrate small progress.

General Strengths Relevant to a Library Setting:
- Comfortable enforcing rules kindly but firmly—for example, asking patrons to lower their voices or step outside to take phone calls, or reminding children to walk instead of run.
- Enjoy learning new systems and procedures. When I started my last job, I took it upon myself to create a quick-reference guide for the point-of-sale system and shared it with new hires.
- Personally passionate about reading and lifelong learning; I read widely (contemporary fiction, historical nonfiction, and middle-grade novels) and often recommend books to friends and family, which I believe would translate well to helping patrons discover new materials.
